credibility_paragraph_full: I have spent twenty-five years building, financing and scaling capital-intensive businesses — from Citigroup's project finance team, where I worked on US$5 billion of infrastructure transactions, through Shell Technology Ventures, to founding Lumicity as a solar and wind developer and serving as Executive Chairman of C-Capture, a carbon capture business backed by IP Group, Drax and BP Ventures. Most recently I founded and ran Fischer Farms, one of the largest vertical farming businesses in the world, for a decade. Since stepping down as CEO earlier this year, I have been approached by a number of companies who have asked me to help them raise capital.
credibility_paragraph_short: Since stepping down at the beginning of this year as CEO of Fischer Farms, one of the largest vertical farming businesses in the world, I have been working with a small number of companies on their fundraising. One of them is SkySails Power.
company_paragraph:
SkySails Power is the world's first commercial airborne wind energy company. Their system uses autonomous kite-like wings tethered to a ground station, flying figure-eight patterns at 200–400m altitude where wind speeds are significantly stronger and more consistent than at conventional turbine height. The result is a mobile, containerised power system that can operate in locations where traditional wind turbines cannot — islands, off-grid mines, disaster relief, and remote industrial sites.

The company has operational units deployed with paying customers, validated energy yields, and a clear commercial pipeline. They are raising €5M as a Series A bridge to scale production and fulfil existing customer commitments.
intelligent_synthesis_template: My understanding is that {{FIRM_NAME}} {{FIRM_THESIS}}. SkySails has a first-mover advantage in commercial airborne wind and a growing pipeline of deployment contracts.

credibility_paragraph_full: I have spent twenty-five years building, financing and scaling capital-intensive businesses — from Citigroup's project finance team, where I worked on US$5 billion of infrastructure transactions, through Shell Technology Ventures, to founding Lumicity as a solar and wind developer and serving as Executive Chairman of C-Capture, a carbon capture business backed by IP Group, Drax and BP Ventures. Most recently I founded and ran Fischer Farms, one of the largest vertical farming businesses in the world, for a decade. Since stepping down as CEO earlier this year, I have been approached by a number of companies who have asked me to help them raise capital.
credibility_paragraph_short: Since stepping down at the beginning of this year as CEO of Fischer Farms, one of the largest vertical farming businesses in the world, I have been working with a small number of companies on their fundraising. One of them is FishFrom Technologies.
company_paragraph:
One of the companies I am working with is FishFrom Technologies, which has developed a photocatalytic ozonation platform — a proprietary advanced oxidation system that eliminates micropollutants and pathogens in recirculating water systems. The immediate application is aquaculture, where every land-based salmon farm loses up to £1.25M per year to depuration (starving fish for 10–15 days to clear off-flavour compounds). FishFrom's compact unit replaces that entirely, in-line, with no fish handling. Beyond aquaculture, the same platform addresses municipal drinking water, EU wastewater treatment (where advanced oxidation is now mandated), cooling tower Legionella control, and food and beverage processing — a combined addressable market in the tens of billions. The business model is rental — £35K per month per unit, hardware payback in six months, 75% gross margin. Swansea University trials complete Q4 2026, with a Mitsubishi-backed beta deployment in Finland following in Q1 2027. They are raising £500K seed (EIS-qualifying), matched by ~£473K in Scottish Enterprise grant funding.

Andrew Robertson (co-founder, FishFrom) has also recorded a short video introduction that explains the technology and the commercial case: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1NaBR14yfBOzrS9GiauCRYDEYs6JpBh7O/view
intelligent_synthesis_template: I am reaching out because my understanding is that {{FIRM_NAME}}'s thesis {{FIRM_THESIS}} — which is exactly where FishFrom lives. The photocatalytic ozonation platform is materials-science-led (advanced oxidation chemistry), IP-protected, and it scales from an acute food-systems problem (aquaculture depuration) into multi-billion-pound adjacent markets.

credibility_paragraph_full: I have spent twenty-five years building, financing and scaling capital-intensive businesses — from Citigroup's project finance team, where I worked on US$5 billion of infrastructure transactions, through Shell Technology Ventures, to founding Lumicity as a solar and wind developer and serving as Executive Chairman of C-Capture, a carbon capture business backed by IP Group, Drax and BP Ventures. Most recently I founded and ran Fischer Farms, one of the largest vertical farming businesses in the world, for a decade. Since stepping down as CEO earlier this year, I have been approached by a number of companies who have asked me to help them raise capital.
credibility_paragraph_short: Since stepping down at the beginning of this year as CEO of Fischer Farms, one of the largest vertical farming businesses in the world, I have been working with a small number of companies on their fundraising. One of them is Panatere.
company_paragraph: One of those is Panatere — a Swiss business led by CEO Andreas Carmichael that uses concentrated solar energy to heat metal crucibles to 2000°C and recycle high-grade alloys (titanium, nickel superalloys, and the full list of 17 EU CRMA strategic metals) with near-zero emissions. The model is effectively a tolling arrangement: they take scrap from Swiss watchmakers, aerospace, defence and medtech customers, melt it down with sunlight, and return certified premium metal to the originator. Customer demand is already running at 30x initial capacity, more than 200 NDAs are signed, EBITDA margins sit above 50%, two patents protect the core crucible and alloy-preserving recycling process, and around CHF 50m of Swiss and EU public support is already in place. They are raising CHF 20–25m for a Series A to build the first industrial solar metallurgy centre in Switzerland.
intelligent_synthesis_template: My understanding is that {{FIRM_NAME}} {{FIRM_THESIS}}. If that is right, Panatere — a patent-protected Swiss industrial platform with strong commercial traction and 50%+ EBITDA margins — may fit your thesis. If you are not the right person internally, I would be very grateful for a pointer to the partner who is closest to this space.

credibility_paragraph_full: I have spent twenty-six years building, financing and scaling capital-intensive businesses — from Citigroup's project finance team, where I worked on US$5 billion of infrastructure transactions, through Shell Technology Ventures, to founding Lumicity as a solar and wind developer and serving as Executive Chairman of C-Capture, a carbon capture business backed by IP Group, Drax and BP Ventures. Most recently I founded and ran Fischer Farms, one of the largest vertical farming businesses in the world, for a decade.
company_paragraph:
Since stepping down as CEO earlier this year, I have launched Fractional Forge — an operating platform for hardware startups. It is, in essence, the wish list of everything I could have had across those twenty-six years. The core problem is simple: a software founder builds one thing; a hardware founder builds two. The company itself, and all the infrastructure around it — manufacturers, engineers, investors, a finance function — all assembled pre-revenue, all without budget.

Fractional Forge is my attempt at the AWS equivalent for hardware. The platform connects founders directly to over 13,700 UK and European manufacturers and 7,800 investors with AI matching. Alongside that there are 13 specialist AI agents across strategy, engineering, finance, legal, manufacturing and the rest, and a marketplace of experienced fractional executives who set their own day rates and can be booked directly. Strategy, objectives, tasks and cash burn projections sit in the same place — because hardware teams always end up distributed across designers, fractional engineers, manufacturers in another country, and a finance advisor in a third.
intelligent_synthesis_template: Given your role {{ROLE_CONTEXT}}, I would like to offer ventures across {{ORGANISATION}}'s {{PIPELINE_DESCRIPTION}} our Starter tier on us — £49/month retail value per venture, free for member companies. The breadth of the partnership is exactly where this kind of horizontal tooling adds the most value: {{CONCRETE_EXAMPLES}} all benefit from the same specialist agents, manufacturer index and fractional executive marketplace, with no per-site setup needed.
